page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 61952.26 and a PassMark CPU score of 57662.99, both placing in the top 25%. The processor's extended instruction set support, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, contributes to strong performance in specialized tasks such as software compilation with Clang and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average performance, with read-only large block transfers at 166.87 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 67.45 GB/sec.

This instance is suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks, as evidenced by its strong PassMark database score of 26359.76 and static web serving throughput of 45.21 GB/sec. The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps network bandwidth provides high resource density for I/O-intensive workloads. This density offers qualitative cost efficiency by reducing the need for external storage attachments, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by its stress-ng multi-core score of 61952.26 and PassMark CPU score of 57662.99. It also exhibits strong capabilities in database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. While it lacks GPU accelerators, the combination of high-speed local storage, dedicated compute resources, and high network throughput makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for I/O-heavy applications. This instance is highly suited for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 platform combining dedicated Intel Xeon compute, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C at 3.5 GHz
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Strong multi-core CPU, database operations, and software compilation benchmarks
Suited for database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing
